On this page

## Syntax[​](#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

![Flow chart showing the syntax of CASE](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

## Description[​](#description "Direct link to Description")

`CASE` goes through a set of conditions and returns a value corresponding to the
first condition met. Each new condition follows the `WHEN condition THEN value`
syntax. The user can define a return value when no condition is met using
`ELSE`. If `ELSE` is not defined and no conditions are met, then case returns
`null`.

## Examples[​](#examples "Direct link to Examples")

Assume the following data

| name | age |
| --- | --- |
| Tom | 4 |
| Jerry | 19 |
| Anna | 25 |
| Jack | 8 |

CASE with ELSE

```prism-code
SELECT  
name,  
CASE  
    WHEN age > 18 THEN 'major'  
    ELSE 'minor'  
END  
FROM my_table
```

Result

| name | case |
| --- | --- |
| Tom | minor |
| Jerry | major |
| Anna | major |
| Jack | minor |

CASE without ELSE

```prism-code
SELECT  
name,  
CASE  
    WHEN age > 18 THEN 'major'  
END  
FROM my_table
```

Result

| name | case |
| --- | --- |
| Tom | null |
| Jerry | major |
| Anna | major |
| Jack | null |
